title: Zambia Is the Tip of the Tail of the Global Dog - Dissident Voice
impact_evidence: Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Anti-corruption and bribery risks -> "These are major mining companies that leech Zambia of its resources through creative means such as transfer mispricing and bribery." || Equity, Rights & Vulnerable Groups | Wealth distribution and inequality | Wealth distribution and inequality -> "Foreign multinational corporations have been the major beneficiaries', he explained. A cozy relationship with the Zambian elites enables these firms to pay low taxes and take their profits out of the country, as well as to use techniques such as outsourcing and subcontracting to skirt Zambia's labour laws." | "in Northern Rhodesia – Zambia's name during colonial rule – two-thirds of the profits were taken out of the territory to pay foreign shareholders, while two-thirds of the remainder went to the European workers and the minuscule leftovers went to the vast majority, the African miners." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "poverty levels in the Copperbelt Province, the heart of Zambia's wealth, are very high." | "Zambia's poverty rate is estimated between 40% and 60% (the country only has statistics up to 2015)." || Social | Living Conditions & Social Fabric | Social infrastructure and amenities (education, healthcare, public services, leisure) -> "The loss of funds from donors to the Ministry of Health in Zambia amounted to US $13.4 million or $1.1 million per year." | "Because of the collapse of the Kwacha between 2015 and 2016, per capita health expenditure in Zambia fell from $44 (2015) to $23 (2016)." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Mining permitting and licensing practices -> "Zambia Consolidated Copper Mines Limited-Investment Holdings (ZCCM-IH), a state-owned corporation, will take a controlling interest in all new mines." || Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Enforcement of environmental and social regulations -> "Mines will have to adhere to state-of-the-art environmental technologies, practices, and standards."

title: Gemfields reports highest auction revenue in five years
impact_evidence: Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation -> "the proceeds of this auction would be fully repatriated to Kagem in Zambia, with all royalties due to Zambia being paid on the full sales prices achieved at the auction." | "said 59 companies had placed bids generating $31.4 million (R445.84m), with an overall average of $115.59 a carat (R1 584.56) during five sequential mini-auctions held between March 15 and April 17." | "Kagem ... generated $11.4m in revenue during its only commercial-quality auction held in February last year, at an average of $4.01 per carat." || Governance | Transparency & Accountability | Corporate transparency and reporting -> "GEMFIELDS, the London-headquartered supplier of coloured gemstones, reported the highest auction revenue in five years from Kagem, its Zambia-based emerald mine." | "The group said its 37 auctions of emeralds and beryl mined at Kagem since July 2009 had generated $688.8m in total revenue."
impact_level3: Income generation and poverty reduction or exacerbation || Corporate transparency and reporting
